goal: Sub 2:30 · Kiprun Singapore Half Marathon · 27 Sep 2026
run_structure: 2 runs per week. Run 1 is ALWAYS easy. Run 2 is the key session (long run or tempo, depending on the phase). Days are flexible — Amanda chooses when she runs. The coach should adapt the plan to whichever days she actually runs, not prescribe specific days.

training_principles.polarized_80_20: With 2 runs/week: one run is ALWAYS easy (HR < easy_max). The other is the key session (long or tempo depending on phase).
training_principles.10_percent_rule: Total weekly km must not increase by more than 10% week-over-week. Flag if exceeded.
training_principles.cutback_weeks: Every 3rd week is a cutback: reduce key session distance by 30% and easy run by 20%. Essential for adaptation.
training_principles.long_run_cap: Key session (long run) should never exceed 55-60% of total weekly volume.
training_principles.recovery: 48-72 hours between runs with only 2x/week is naturally built in. If RHR is elevated >5 bpm above baseline, suggest extra rest.
training_principles.heat_adjustment: Singapore heat adds 10-15 bpm to HR. Pace should be guided by HR, not speed. If HR exceeds zone, slow down — never push through.
training_principles.rpe_guidance: Easy runs: RPE 3-4 (can hold a conversation). Tempo: RPE 6-7 (can speak in short sentences). Long runs: RPE 4-5 (comfortable but purposeful).
training_principles.flexible_scheduling: Amanda picks her run days. If she has done 0 runs this week and it is late in the week, gently encourage getting one in. If she has done 1, suggest the second. If she has done 2, the rest of the week is recovery. Never guilt-trip about missed days.

injury_notes.note: Slight pain in left shin after Night Run. Monitor for shin splints — reduce volume if it persists, suggest calf raises and shin stretches, avoid hard surfaces.

illness.coaching_override: CRITICAL — this overrides all training plans. Influenza A is a systemic viral illness with myocarditis risk. Do NOT prescribe any running until: (1) fever-free for at least 3 full days WITHOUT medication, AND (2) all below-the-neck symptoms (body aches, chest congestion, fatigue) fully resolved. Apply the 'neck check' rule: any symptoms below the neck = no exercise. Even light exercise during/right after flu can cause dangerous heart complications.
illness.return_protocol: Once cleared: start with GENTLE WALKS only for the first few days, not running. Then very easy short runs (3-4 km, HR < 140, walk breaks encouraged) at ~50% of previous volume. Increase by no more than 10-15% per week. Full training intensity should not resume for 1-2 weeks after symptoms clear. Expect elevated HR and lower energy for a while — this is normal post-viral and NOT a fitness loss to worry about.
illness.tone: Be extra gentle and reassuring. She may feel anxious about lost training time before the Sep 27 race — reassure her that there is ample time to rebuild, that rushing back risks far more setback than a patient recovery, and that her body fighting the virus IS the priority right now. Health first, always. The race will still be there.

hr_zones.notes: Singapore heat adds 10-15 bpm vs cool conditions. All HR targets already adjusted for heat. Guide by HR, not pace — pace will vary day to day.

training_phases.name: Base Building
training_phases.focus: Build aerobic base and running habit. All runs easy (HR < 150). No tempo work yet.

training_phases.name: Build Phase 1
training_phases.focus: Introduce tempo segments. Long run extends. 80/20 polarization begins.
training_phases.key_session: Alternating: Long 12 km (easy) OR Tempo 8 km (2 km warm-up, 4 km @ 7:00-7:15/km, 2 km cool-down)

training_phases.name: Pre-Travel Taper
training_phases.focus: Reduce volume by 40%. One short tempo. Stay fresh for Norway.

training_phases.name: Norway Hiking
training_phases.focus: Major active cross-training block. 9 hike days across 14 days. All hikes rated Hard. Total ~83 km, ~6,059 m elevation gain. This is equivalent to a high-altitude training camp — the aerobic and muscular stimulus is enormous. No running needed or expected.
training_phases.coach_notes: Aug 9-11 are 3 consecutive hard days — the most physically demanding stretch. Aug 12-13 and 16 are natural rest days. The hiking will build significant leg strength, aerobic capacity, and mental toughness that directly transfers to the half marathon. Expect legs to feel heavy in the first week back running.
training_phases.pre_norway_prep: In Build Phase 1 (Jul 19 - Aug 1), incorporate hills into runs where possible. Mention that Norway elevation training is a huge advantage for the race.

training_phases.name: Return to Running
training_phases.focus: Ease back in after 9 hard hike days and long-haul travel. Legs have done enormous work (83 km, 6,059 m elevation) and need time to transition back to road running. Do NOT rush.
training_phases.easy_run: 4-5 km very gentle, walk breaks fully OK. HR < 145.
training_phases.key_session: 6 km easy — no tempo yet. Legs will feel different from hiking vs running. Trust the process.
training_phases.coach_notes: If legs feel unusually heavy or dead in the first 2 runs back, that is normal post-hiking DOMS. Do not increase pace or distance until running feels fluid again. The Norway fitness gains will show up in weeks 2-3 post-return.

training_phases.name: Peak Block
training_phases.focus: Race-specific training. Long runs reach 18 km. Tempo at race pace (7:06/km). Hardest 3 weeks.
training_phases.easy_run: 6 km easy OR 6 km tempo (alternate weeks)
training_phases.key_session_progression: Wk1: Long 14 km. Wk2: Long 16 km. Wk3 (cutback): Long 12 km.

training_phases.name: Race Taper
training_phases.focus: Reduce volume 50%. 1-2 short race-pace strides. Rest legs, trust the training. Carb-load Thu-Sat.

training_phases.name: RACE DAY
training_phases.focus: Sub 2:30. Start conservative (7:15-7:20/km first 5 km), settle into 7:00-7:06/km, negative split if possible. Hydrate every 3 km.
